question.text: Open position limits at the client level for commodity index futures are:

answer.text: 5% of open interest or 1,000 lots, whichever is higher
10% of open interest or 2,000 lots, whichever is higher
15% of open interest or 10,000 lots, whichever is higher
3% of open interest or 500 lots, whichever is higher

explanation.text: **The Logic:** At client level, position limits for index futures are 5% of open interest or 1,000 lots (whichever is higher). At member level, it is 15% of OI or 10,000 lots. **The Trap:** 10%/2,000 lots is for index OPTIONS (double of futures). 15%/10,000 is member level, not client. 3%/500 is not a specified limit. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers position limits for equity F&O. Series I covers currency derivatives position limits. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Position limits for index options are double that of index futures — 10% OI/2,000 lots at client level. This higher limit reflects the lower risk profile of option buyers.
